![Observed Early LPP and predictions from four selected pooled source-context models. Each model row groups recalled and unrecalled bars for Negative and Neutral items on one shared axis. Negative items are red and Neutral items are black, matching the empirical figure convention; recalled bars are filled and unrecalled bars are outlined. Observed error bars are percentile 95% confidence intervals from 10,000 participant-level bootstrap samples ($N=38$). Predicted error bars are central 95% intervals across 200 complete simulated datasets and represent simulation variability rather than parameter-estimation uncertainty. Simulations condition on each list's observed recall count. The LPP-by-recall-status pattern is a derived diagnostic rather than an independently optimized target or out-of-sample validation.](early_lpp_diagnostic_figure.svg){#fig-model-early-lpp width="100%" fig-alt="Five model rows each group four Early-LPP bars: recalled and unrecalled Negative items in red, followed by recalled and unrecalled Neutral items in black. Observed data show a large recalled-minus-unrecalled separation for Negative items and little separation for Neutral items. Categorical enhancement alone produces little separation, while the LPP models reproduce the Negative-item pattern to varying degrees."}
